blocks.title: Attribution, up front
blocks.text: A2UI (Agent2UI) is an open, Apache 2.0-licensed protocol **created by Google**, with contributions from CopilotKit and the open source community. Its home is a2ui.org; the reference repository is github.com/a2ui-project/a2ui. a2uicatalog.ai is an independent, third-party catalog and renderer built *on* this protocol — it does not define it.

The problem A2UI solves is specific: **how can an AI agent safely send a rich, interactive UI across a trust boundary?** Two bad answers dominate practice — ship raw HTML/JS (arbitrary code execution risk) or restrict agents to plain text (no UI at all). A2UI's answer: the agent sends **declarative component descriptions** — JSON, never code — and the client renders them using its own native, pre-approved widgets.

points: Security first: the client owns a trusted catalog of components (Card, Button, TextField…); the agent can only reference components in that catalog, never inject arbitrary markup or scripts
Structure and data are deliberately separated — a component tree and a data model, updated independently
The protocol is JSON-based and designed for streaming: a renderer can begin drawing before the full payload has arrived

blocks.title: Version reality — it's not finished
blocks.text: As of this writing, A2UI's current *production* release is **v0.9.1** (stable). **v1.0 is a release candidate** — not yet stable, still open to breaking changes if enough implementer feedback demands it. a2uicatalog's own protocol emitter targets v1.0. Building against a candidate spec is a real, live tradeoff worth knowing, not a footnote.

a2uicatalog.ai is a **catalog implementation**: 468 typed UI atoms, a renderer that runs them on eight surfaces, and a simpler JSON authoring shape — `{title, theme, blocks: [...]}`, blocks keyed by `type` — that this whole curriculum has been using. That shape is **not** the A2UI wire protocol. It's a convenience dialect the catalogue converts *into* real, conformant A2UI v1.0 `createSurface` messages via its own `a2ui-emit` tool.

items.label: Author
items.text: Write in the catalogue's blocks dialect — the shape used throughout this brief

items.label: Convert
items.text: a2ui-emit walks the blocks and produces a real, spec-conformant createSurface message

items.label: Render
items.text: Any A2UI-conformant client that understands the referenced catalog can render the result — not just a2uicatalog's own renderer

blocks.question: Who created the A2UI protocol?
options: a2uicatalog.ai, which later opened it up
Google, as an open Apache 2.0 project with outside contributions
A vendor-neutral standards body with no single author
It emerged from the MCP specification
blocks.explanation: A2UI is Google-created and Apache 2.0 licensed. a2uicatalog is a downstream, independent implementation — this distinction is the whole point of this module.
